status: partial landed batch after pivot; remaining queued variants intentionally stopped once structural signal was clear

branch_design_rationale.why_old_basin_is_not_enough: the old post-unify family only moved max inside the 99.6x band, so it is not a credible route to sub-50
branch_design_rationale.new_family: distributed late-block full cycles across anchors 9/10/11
branch_design_rationale.scientific_hypothesis: if Ka2_y is limited by local observability saturation, repeating the same continuity-safe sign-separated Y exposure across multiple consecutive late z-family anchor states should lower the ceiling more qualitatively than one-point dose tweaks
branch_design_rationale.mechanism_rule: every landed candidate uses only legal real dual-axis actions and reconnects exactly before the next base action

landed_batch.rationale: Leave the old local basin and place the same full continuity-safe late-block cycle at both anchors 10 and 11. Theory: repeated sign-separated late Y exposure at two distinct late z-family faces may attack the Ka2_y ceiling more structurally than any single-anchor dose tweak.

Push the structural idea further: distribute the full late-block cycle across anchors 9, 10, and 11 so the weak late segment is excited at three consecutive z-family base states rather than one local pocket.

Same distributed double-block idea, but use the stronger mean-oriented full block at both 10 and 11 to test whether multi-anchor redistribution can keep the structure stable while attacking Ka2_y more strongly.
Lighter triple-block sibling. Keeps the same three-anchor redistribution but reduces each full-block Y dwell to see whether the ceiling move comes from distribution itself rather than raw dwell size.

best_new_candidate.rationale: Leave the old local basin and place the same full continuity-safe late-block cycle at both anchors 10 and 11. Theory: repeated sign-separated late Y exposure at two distinct late z-family faces may attack the Ka2_y ceiling more structurally than any single-anchor dose tweak.

best_new_lowmax_candidate.rationale: Leave the old local basin and place the same full continuity-safe late-block cycle at both anchors 10 and 11. Theory: repeated sign-separated late Y exposure at two distinct late z-family faces may attack the Ka2_y ceiling more structurally than any single-anchor dose tweak.

bottom_line.branch_verdict: best landed distributed branch `redistribute_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0` changed mean/max by -0.003 / -0.561 versus the unified winner; the best landed max in this family was 100.194, still worse than the current winner (99.633) and far above 50.
bottom_line.sub50_plausibility: not plausible from the landed structural batch: distributed late-block candidates reached best max=100.194, with Ka2_y still ~100.194. The branch attacked dKg_zz, but did not break the Ka2_y ceiling qualitatively.

scientific_conclusion: The pivot answered the strategic question. Leaving the old post-unify basin and redistributing continuity-safe full late-block cycles across anchors 9/10/11 did create a genuinely different legal family, but the landed batch still gave max values of 100.194, 113.725, 105.059, 116.434. The best landed point was redistribute_l10_l11_fullblock_y8x0 at 9.926 / 1.373 / 100.194. So the ceiling is not just a tiny late10/late11 local-dose issue, but under the current hard constraints this more aggressive distributed branch still does not make sub-50 look realistic.
